**They offer free ultrasounds to women in crisis.** They meet them where they are. They show them the reality of life inside the womb. **They walk with those women for up to two years after the baby is born.** **They share the Gospel.** They do not abandon women. They stay. Here is what stopped me. It costs just $28 to provide a free ultrasound. That is it.
$28 to help save a baby's life.
$140 to help rescue five. **[_Click here to give now_ _to support this incredible work >>_](https://email.updates.conservativeinnovation.com/c/eJykkc-K3DAMxp9Guc1gy4m9OeSwbDbQwyw9FHoMHluTmMZ_sJ0MffuSMn2C3qRPHz9JfD9v8027zYXlS3sazOHmXObnqmtdSd9jri4GF-xeav69xhkZdhfOL4yfvhBTpnvMYeaNHYyVAhsauJK87zm2slkHi93bQzwY9owb6tWdS6WVIWK2VW2nGjecTM4Z54wxoa6ia_HR6TfDW2LCWGjZnqyuVK4mhkL50NUd5EKIZxXD1UTfbMNaayog3gEnwGlxB11f150GwOnVAU46JdJbATEVA2L88cEkdt8_bwJQal9BjB0DlEsBMRZAuVc_l7hnQyDGlGNJZKoLy2vkybrdgxjJa7e9RKN90m4JIMa6m1-U_-kxVArnjvfPy8e3L9Uy2Td5sOnpts0VqaBlywn6-1f9n0yOAf8EAAD__5nen0s)_ _** You and I both know the abortion industry has everything on its side. The media. The government. The money. The silence of people who should speak up. But we still have the truth.
We still have a voice.
And we still have the ability to act. If you want to fight back against a culture of death, this is how you do it. In a real and practical way. Support PreBorn!

Help save a life today. Sincerely,
Tucker Carlson

subject: SNAP Payments, Dodgers Force Game 7, and a Lincoln Bathroom Re-Do
body:
Two federal judges ruled on Friday that the government [must continue to fund SNAP](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL3O3CAURJ8Gd6zg-ge7oEjjbtOmtFi42KwwWHC9K7995Eh5gq8baaTRnPPnuTxNiCGtv82OmnxeEn5rRCIsS03mOMy1Y6LqsluxVJ-LxXUBAT2Xkgu5OBPitXSN0x4RsG1QSzXIaZIdjM2mJzX0Vnjh1TR106h6MwwKjHLoO4s9NEHfa1IKKYVou_7xAmFRCN-q9g7AOoEuUC6Pdw6JNvTxyh8sD5v3JuqN6Kis_cVgZjAP5mXvgsF85EoM5vfpVqw8F4eFUzn3gxu3hxQqFUMhJ35W5DYnCmnFZC_uz-Qqv-n5f3zuzhLSyut2ksvfxGCWo5j6sR8YzE3R7viGGEMdFOvEupsQ_92jHzn9aPgbAAD__1HVjQo), the nation's biggest food aid program, using contingency funds during the government shutdown.

# Saturday, November 1, 2025
---|---
**Good morning!** On this day in 1765, [the Stamp Act](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDtuhTAURFdjOiN_-BYu0tC9tCnRBV-DI2Mj--ohdh8RZQfpRjrSjOZ8veYX-ODj9gkHGnJpjniVgESY5xLhPOE-MFKxyW6Yi0t5xW1WQrVcSi7kbMGHe24qaxyiQl2hkX0nx1E2aqh203ZtK8CNC7hhHLCXGpamw2HsOqvkoitvnjYphZRC6KatFyVWFMLpXj9BsUag9ZRy_Z18pB1duNMbc72mowpmJzoL0x9MTUxN13XVuy-U8v1wpibafeEWbu4j_yNMTTG98Vgwc8nUdEIOHp6fHCOsVDjtyAvBcXJYqcrGnpcPwZeuZ43YDvDhd53-pext1E8AAAD__xHVgc8) was enacted to raise revenue for British military operations in America. However, its direct tax on colonists leads to widespread outrage, over the issue of taxation without representation.

It's that time of year again: Tonight ([technically at 1:59 a.m. local time Sunday morning](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0L-OtCAUBfCnwQ7DH5nRguJr7OZrtzRXuCo7CAZYiW-_cbNvsN0vucnNOefjNb3AeRfW_7CjLkucAtbssRRMUw5wHHDtGEq20a6Y8hKTwXUSTCjKOWV8suD8NXWN1QuiQNmg5s8HHwbeib7ZtJIDMDYrJYwyD1SSg7Vghx74DL2xjdP3N84Z54zJTrWzYAYZW-RT3hCkY2hdian9jC6UDRd_xRNTa-LeeL2VcmQi_xExEjHWWtswm7vFfSdivEnE-JXpryxc3q1boRlOF1Za3I4Ug820blBoifQdYqXGR_POdAHv6QzmTZMJIDo2DH2TtD2q897lx5N0bN3B-Z845U8bnlp8BwAA__8sG4ef)), we bid farewell to daylight saving time. Don't forget to set your clocks back one hour when you go to sleep, and be prepared for it to start getting dark at night very early!

Wyoming is on the verge of a clean-energy revolution, and today's sponsor, [Vero3](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLtq7DAQhp9G7mx08bVQcWDZbk-bUsjW2J6gG5LsZd8-eBPyAumG__-YGb6Ph3potOi3_9qBLGtQHp7ZQimQVPY6Rv1y4Es2wWyQ8hrSApvilHc1YzVlymi0L9VWRq4AHEQFkg09mybW8rHa5TSM86Ln3nR8FatuNaeGGjPBNIu503OF8trGGGWMUtF2zczpApSuYhDXwElLwWAJqfkM6MsOq32FE1KzBFdZuZcSMxH_CL8Tfkd_Qi7NCSmIC7hScT-KUzkcaQEibuA0WsL7K3Rg8HBE3KJGU0edioeUd4w__aJd1Lj5N_FbspHWohPq28DhsWgl2pFR2rOx66okTXyitZj7gbR0uw6-vy1_UnxK_hUAAP__ni6Qhw) is leading the charge—turning lithium extraction into a zero-waste process that also creates clean water and captures carbon, with public investment open ahead of its planned 2028 Nasdaq IPO.

## Court Orders SNAP Payments
Two federal judges ruled on Friday that the government [must continue to fund SNAP](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL3O3CAURJ8Gd6zg-ge7oEjjbtOmtFi42KwwWHC9K7995Eh5gq8baaTRnPPnuTxNiCGtv82OmnxeEn5rRCIsS03mOMy1Y6LqsluxVJ-LxXUBAT2Xkgu5OBPitXSN0x4RsG1QSzXIaZIdjM2mJzX0Vnjh1TR106h6MwwKjHLoO4s9NEHfa1IKKYVou_7xAmFRCN-q9g7AOoEuUC6Pdw6JNvTxyh8sD5v3JuqN6Kis_cVgZjAP5mXvgsF85EoM5vfpVqw8F4eFUzn3gxu3hxQqFUMhJ35W5DYnCmnFZC_uz-Qqv-n5f3zuzhLSyut2ksvfxGCWo5j6sR8YzE3R7viGGEMdFOvEupsQ_92jHzn9aPgbAAD__1HVjQo), the nation's biggest food aid program, using contingency funds during the government shutdown. The rulings came a day before the U.S. Department of Agriculture planned to freeze payments to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program because it said it could no longer keep funding it due to the shutdown. In separate cases, [federal judges in Rhode Island](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkD2OgzAUhE9jOtDzA0MoXGxDl223RI79DI78g2wnKLdfsVfYbqSRvtF8P_f1rpx3cftWgWS1aY10Fk-1Ul5LVMehPoFiLSaZjXKxKWvaVgQULect8NUo5z_r0BhpiZD6hiSfRj7PfMBbs8sRpxtyGqywMMOkZ_G4aaOEHudRCOgbJy8a58A5QD-I7oGgCcD2U38FZAOQcTXl7plcrDtZ_0lvyp1OofFyr_UorP9iuDBczvPsdHzoq2S4XGiGCweGS88ZLtentuZXONrny2zU2pRMW6oKR2nL_qomnbHba_BNluY4nfeujBMbYAvK-b_N-i9Rb4m_AQAA__87gnt3) and Massachusetts said the Trump administration must use contingency funds to keep at least partial payments going. Both courts asked for updates on government action by Monday. The administration is expected to appeal the orders. In the meantime, a number of states have announced local programs to help food stamp recipients. Here’s a list of [what each state is planning](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1uhDAQhJ8GOqO1-bmjcJGG7tKmRAavYSNjI7x3zr195LxCupFGM5pvvh7zw5CnsH2aAzW7OAfMySMzXnMK5jzN-8DAyUa74ZVcvFbcZgWqF1IKkLM15N9zV1vtEBW2NWp5G-Q4yk7d6123cF_aXt3N4kY3rKBMB240dlmGDtxga9KlTUqQEqDt-mZRsCKAa29tEarqAC1xvJrvSIF3dP4dX3g1azxqr3fmM1XtR6WmSk0558bFn96uxa7UVHgqNRUWsWBAR5zEVvKhgIm0P9nGHIR7ei88JRZ5NywSG8YkbKSwiR39KZw5yBOm-tL2zOQ9peFWdbAdhvzfGP7Xgy-tfgMAAP__yTaIvg).
## FBI Thwarts Terror Attack
The FBI arrested multiple people in Michigan, [thwarting a potential terrorist attack](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkL1u7CAUhJ8Gd1iA_9YFxW3c7W1TWhgO9kkwIDhZa98-cl4h1XzSSKOZ-XiuT4MB4_7fnKDJpzXCVQMQQVlrNDmb9wmRqktuh1J9Khb2VQk1cCm5kKszGN5r3zjtARR0DWg5jXKeZa8ezaHFQzkx9lbC5Ac_ej-5QfYSJjPPm--2BvWdJqWQUoiuH9pNCQtC-G7qblCsF-CQUmk_E0Y6wId3ekFpbTqboA-iXFn3j6mFqeW6rtZu9V5x-0wtNzK1-A15TgSR0AROUEoqWIkbImO_OB2XKQSOn2gP3E3k53cgzAF4hnSLKQUqgWNqaYp2-cIQsI4T68V-Ggy_dehPH760-gkAAP__3nSJaA) over Halloween weekend, FBI Director Kash Patel said on Friday morning. Five people between the ages of 16 and 20 were arrested Friday. Authorities say the plan was inspired by a former Michigan National Guard member who was arrested in May for allegedly planning as ISIS-inspired attack in Detroit. Patel, in a social media post, said that other details about the arrests would be forthcoming. The [Dearborn Police Department](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJyk0D1uxCAUBODT4A4LHv7bgiKNu02b0sLwbEgwWPC01t4-cq6QbqSRPo3m67k8TYgh7Z_mQE1bXhJeNSIRlqUmc57mfWCi6rLbsdQtF4v7AgJ6LiUXcnEmxPfSNU5viICqQS3HQT4esoOp8VqsOEk19MKNFoWz0wj9sG6jQjd11kIT9K1JKaQUQnV9u4KwKMSmRnUHYJ1AFyiX9juHRB63-M4vLK3NRxO1JzorUx8MZgbzdV2tTau9SwbzTTOYpWAwK8lg3tbAf0z1_DSEkR_B-rCbxAlLySVU4t7EmC_ExE0pWKm2no7YFO3OK8QY6jCyTuyHCfFvAf3rtpeG3wAAAP__8iqBgA) said on Facebook that it had been “made aware that the FBI conducted operations in the City of Dearborn earlier this morning” and assured residents there was no current threat to the community.
## Trump Urges End to Filibuster
President Trump called on GOP leaders [to use the “nuclear option”](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkMGOrCAQRb8GdxgosG0XLN7GXb_tLA1KoUwQDJRt-u8nzi_MrpKTurnnfr2mlw0xpPW_3dGQz1PCq0YkwjLVZI_DfnZMVF12K5bqc1lwnUBAx6XkQk7OhviZdOOMRwRUDRrZP-QwSA3PZjNPO3jfgVK4CJh7KRV0QkuhhdB2AGyCudOkFFIKoXTXziAWFMKrXt0HMC3QBcql_c4h0YY-fvIbS7vkvYlmIzoqU_8YjAzG67raZa63xc0ZjDG8kZ-Hs4SVwbjer-l24nU7yeUr8Xgz4lTO_eAVkyXkBY9zjmGxqXIfYpjPSlgYjE0x7rhCjKE-eqbFutsQf7vQnwZ8G_gJAAD__0i-h5E) and kill the Senate filibuster in order to end the government shutdown with a simple majority vote, but met resistance in both chambers. Senate Majority Leader John Thune remains strongly opposed to ending the filibuster, a spokesman said Friday, and House Speaker Mike Johnson [pointed out the potential dangers](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkDGu4yAYhE-DO6IfDHZSUGzjLttuaWH4MSQYvIAT5fZPzhVe941GmtHMv_t81yGGtP7VG6rm8pzwXSO2hmWuSe-7_myYWrXZrliqy8XgOnPgkjJGgc1Wh_iZRWeVQ-TYd6jYOLDbjQl-7bxatDBSoHVG9wMaeRsHuLpBLrBIo0fogjrTGAPGAHohLwsHgwCuH_sTOBGANrRcLo8cUvPo4ie_sFxM3rqofGt7Jf0fwifCp-bRhxhPj_DJ5w3PQV88KhI-SXntGQhad9RPLPSRfao50bcuqdLsqMmp4v8Dk8GvTsczpJW6EMNy1IaF8Kkryu7vEGOow0gErJsO386u_erEl-I_AQAA__-frYgr) of ending the long-standing Senate tradition. Johnson noted that the filibuster, which requires 60 votes to approve some measures, has protected both the Republican and Democratic parties when they’ve been in the minority.

| _Flying together with our sponsor_ [](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLtq7DAQhp9G7mx08bVQcWDZbk-bUsjW2J6gG5LsZd8-eBPyAumG__-YGb6Ph3potOi3_9qBLGtQHp7ZQimQVPY6Rv1y4Es2wWyQ8hrSApvilHc1YzVlymi0L9VWRq4AHEQFkg09mybW8rHa5TSM86Ln3nR8FatuNaeGGjPBNIu503OF8trGGGWMUtF2zczpApSuYhDXwElLwWAJqfkM6MsOq32FE1KzBFdZuZcSMxH_CL8Tfkd_Qi7NCSmIC7hScT-KUzkcaQEibuA0WsL7K3Rg8HBE3KJGU0edioeUd4w__aJd1Lj5N_FbspHWohPq28DhsWgl2pFR2rOx66okTXyitZj7gbR0uw6-vy1_UnxK_hUAAP__ni6Qhw) **How Wyoming Will Become a Lithium Powerhouse** America needs 5X more lithium by 2035, but no new mines have opened since 1967. Meanwhile, current extraction methods are slow, waste tons of water, and emit loads of carbon. So [Vero3](https://email.editor.jointheflyover.com/c/eJykkLtq7DAQhp9G7mx08bVQcWDZbk-bUsjW2J6gG5LsZd8-eBPyAumG__-YGb6Ph3potOi3_9qBLGtQHp7ZQimQVPY6Rv1y4Es2wWyQ8hrSApvilHc1YzVlymi0L9VWRq4AHEQFkg09mybW8rHa5TSM86Ln3nR8FatuNaeGGjPBNIu503OF8trGGGWMUtF2zczpApSuYhDXwElLwWAJqfkM6MsOq32FE1KzBFdZuZcSMxH_CL8Tfkd_Qi7NCSmIC7hScT-KUzkcaQEibuA0WsL7K3Rg8HBE3KJGU0edioeUd4w__aJd1Lj5N_FbspHWohPq28DhsWgl2pFR2rOx66okTXyitZj7gbR0uw6-vy1_UnxK_hUAAP__ni6Qhw) devised a way to not only cleanly produce 9K tons of lithium, they’ll generate 2.3B gallons of clean water and permanently store 3M tons of carbon in the process—all from the same 14,000-acre Wyoming site. clean_body: Two federal judges ruled on Friday that the government , the nation's biggest food aid program, using contingency funds during the government shutdown. | | | | | | Saturday, November 1, 2025 ---|--- Good morning! On this day in 1765, was enacted to raise revenue for British military operations in America. However, its direct tax on colonists leads to widespread outrage, over the issue of taxation without representation. It's that time of year again: Tonight (), we bid farewell to daylight saving time. Don't forget to set your clocks back one hour when you go to sleep, and be prepared for it to start getting dark at night very early! Wyoming is on the verge of a clean-energy revolution, and today's sponsor, is leading the charge—turning lithium extraction into a zero-waste process that also creates clean water and captures carbon, with public investment open ahead of its planned 2028 Nasdaq IPO. | Court Orders SNAP Payments Two federal judges ruled on Friday that the government , the nation's biggest food aid program, using contingency funds during the government shutdown. The rulings came a day before the U.S. Department of Agriculture planned to freeze payments to the Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program because it said it could no longer keep funding it due to the shutdown. In separate cases, and Massachusetts said the Trump administration must use contingency funds to keep at least partial payments going. Both courts asked for updates on government action by Monday. The administration is expected to appeal the orders. In the meantime, a number of states have announced local programs to help food stamp recipients. Here’s a list of . FBI Thwarts Terror Attack The FBI arrested multiple people in Michigan, over Halloween weekend, FBI Director Kash Patel said on Friday morning. Five people between the ages of 16 and 20 were arrested Friday. Authorities say the plan was inspired by a former Michigan National Guard member who was arrested in May for allegedly planning as ISIS-inspired attack in Detroit. Patel, in a social media post, said that other details about the arrests would be forthcoming. The said on Facebook that it had been “made aware that the FBI conducted operations in the City of Dearborn earlier this morning” and assured residents there was no current threat to the community. Trump Urges End to Filibuster President Trump called on GOP leaders and kill the Senate filibuster in order to end the government shutdown with a simple majority vote, but met resistance in both chambers. Senate Majority Leader John Thune remains strongly opposed to ending the filibuster, a spokesman said Friday, and House Speaker Mike Johnson of ending the long-standing Senate tradition. Johnson noted that the filibuster, which requires 60 votes to approve some measures, has protected both the Republican and Democratic parties when they’ve been in the minority. | The following stories are featured exclusively on The Flyover Podcast—a daily show that gives you the most important headlines in under 15 minutes, straight from the heart of the country.Clicking the link will take you directly to these stories: ➤ The U.S. halted 13 Mexican airline routes after accusing Mexico of violating a key aviation agreement. **Good Morning!** On this day in 1898, blues singer [Beulah Thomas "Sippie" Wallace](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLGSrCAURL9GMy1AVAwIXmI2L93QushVbi2CBazO_P3WzC9s0FlX96nz9VgeQJ7C_h8O1OW5xSXgnT2WgmnZDG2UKOwFn5CvfEGwLwgWFsFE33DeML5YIP9aZG21YXbiNWo-Dnya5MRl7bSRSplhNGJTnLN1MHbseyaVYqrvcTQ16fcY54y_03ft1HWbkasSBlWvVqwkQ0slprY4_JBs_hUvTO0aj9prV8qZq-5fJeZKzPd9tyU7iMFTwDamvRKzg2BNjN-VmDGURJjfTfAeVmwM_nhwTXHxgNxkOk_COml73uQ95WGsJNsPIP_5K3-zdGnxGwAA___2qX2j) was born in Houston. Among her hits are _Mighty Tight Woman_ and _Women Be Wise_ , both of which were covered by Bonnie Raitt in her 1971 debut album. It's November, which means some of you might have been up in a tree stand before the _Texas Flyover_ hit your inbox this morning. ## San Antonio FBI Head Dismissed
Aaron Tapp, the head of the FBI’s San Antonio field office, was [fired Friday](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkMGK5CAQQL_G3GzUmO7OwUOW3dxmWdjDHENFy6TAaFA3mf77Jf0LcygoeFTxeJ8f0wdQoLj8hg1N_fJpiniWgLVinvxMnjLFpeIXlKMcEN0LooNJCdVxKbmQkwMKr0k3zszC9bJBIx932fe6l7pZjQawSoNUz85LrzoNDrSfvboLe--laMhcz6QU8pquvfVt62dtn2rGZ_e0yLRARzXlW13xbeLDKx2YbzZtTTBrrXth7cDUyNR4nudtK_FiTI0Y-b_C1LinQJXstS7XadwwVqbGjHvKlYPbKFKpGSqlyD1lLHxFcDx57mfihReIHGJNkRL3hOFCniwyNULmwyD_DI9fn6wdkyXH2p8_rqh4lr-Y9yYbt58UApX7g2mxbEDhbV-_1_ww6n8AAAD__3oPmLE) for reasons yet to be disclosed by the agency. According to reports, the firing allegedly stemmed from Tapp’s role in the investigation of Republican leaders seeking to overturn the results of the 2020 presidential election. GOP lawmakers recently released records from the investigation, dubbed “[Arctic Frost](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkDtuwzAMQE9jbwos-RcNGrp4S9eOBmVRDgFZMiTWSW5fOCjQA3TgQjw8PPLrNt-AAsX1EzY0_PRpjvgoAZkxz96Sp0xxZXxCOcoB0b0gOphVo3ohpWjk7IDCa-5qZ2zjtKzRyHGQWndadvXdjGh7ZRcHTg-2s1oudrReOTlKvQyNr8mcMikbeU7fXnTbetstV2Xx2l8XrLoGHXHKF77ju8SHVzowX5a01cHcmfdStR-Vmio1-fQsECFyipROolLT30niXVupqUAUv5DwlkTZcSEIAlaMLE57QaS4ij1jIXcuOX9vu6B4YGFagSnFIjxldHU2bn9QCFSGseqadQMK7zr-308Po34CAAD__6NMkZI),” which was terminated after President Trump won reelection in 2024. Tapp is a 22-year veteran of the FBI and took charge of the San Antonio field office in 2024.
## Texas Faces Vandy in Top-20 Game
The No. 20 Texas Longhorns hosts the No, 9 Vanderbilt Commodores today at 11:00 in a [top-20 matchup](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkLHupCAQh58GOzYwIqsFxTV0_2uvNAiDkiBsgOj69hfNvcEVk0xmMpPv9_35mX9MiCGtv82Oqn19nhOeNWJrWGa_BB9KSGvDr6lHPUxyl0nOzMBgoJxTxmdnQrxm0Tm1MDfxDhV_Sz5NYuKi29RoUFgAPrHey_Hdj1xKb7gYmB0Ga2UX1P2Mc8bvGvrX1Pd-EXaEBcdhtEgEQxdaLq-24UPi45UPLC-b9y6qrbVPJf0vApqAPs_zhfWT7iUBfbcE9IKthbQS0LXlchHQMwEdHAEt5Fv2DAYC2uYYcUXqc26LiZH-O6NP3C2f94DeFrAsITZq875nlwtW-oDRmNO65ZJqV5T7nCHGUOWbCLbuJsQHuP2f5kPB3wAAAP__rKWScw) with college football playoff implications. ## SpaceX Defends Lunar Lander, Wins Funding
Texas-based SpaceX is [defending](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkL9uwyAYxJ_G3ogMxn8YGLpkS9eO1mf4iD8VAwLqJG9fOVKfoMMNp5NOv7uv23ID8hTun7Cjrk8Xl4CP4rFWzItbyVGmcK_4hHKUA4J9QbCwiE4MjHPW8cUC-dciW6vXzireoubTyJWSist202aaOfBphLXns0QxDEoBGqfWCd04iZb0WcZ5x08N_UX1vVulmcWK8zAbbGSHlmrMl7rhm8T5VzwwX0zcW6-3WlNp-o9GXBtxLQkMnhvO9M8_mUWHwRZWKuSyUWL-J0BmHoLFzKAwquwR83dhMbBCe_LkCC2DlHIEszXi2mZt04O8pzJOjezuO5B_M9T_PXdo8RsAAP__6kyFyg) its moon program in response to recent criticism from the NASA community, announcing its intention to develop a “simplified” lunar landing mission architecture. SpaceX officials said the simpler approach should speed up the return to the moon, answering NASA's call to provide “acceleration approaches” to Artemis 3, the lunar mission currently contracted to SpaceX. Meanwhile, SpaceX is expecting to [receive $2 billion](https://email.editor.thetexasflyover.com/c/eJykkEGu3CAMhk9DdowCZMJkwaKqlG7e66JS1WUEwclYQyAFv8zk9hVzhS4sWfpk-_P_53P6tBgwrj_tBoZeS5oiPEsAIsjT4nDBjHEleNlylMNGf9ro7SRbeeVC8FZM3mI4p67xxrV-EA0YoXsxDN0guuZuencdtAbV-965QQlYhFVSC620tE7pBk1dJkQral3VZVBqcd18kw5u19sMrGvBI6V8oTu8TZZwpgPyZU5bE8ydaC9MfWNyZHIslOYHPZFKxUyO9R9uM-EcoDA5bjY_gGoHf7-Qzjqz2xlevABxStzZlUvH13oibhCJL1_RY1wr9HBASDtfU_AQuU8b8GIJQkCCwjPsKROT4_zxY399f_xSvz-abPz-xBCw9Jp17bpZDG93-r_EDyP_BQAA___IXZl8) in government funding to develop satellites for President Trump’s Golden Dome project, highlighting the company’s expanding role in U.S. defense. SpaceX is also reportedly expected to be a major player in two other contracts with the Pentagon involving satellites, one for relaying sensitive military communications and another for tracking vehicles on the ground.

GOP lawmakers recently released records from the investigation, dubbed “,” which was terminated after President Trump won reelection in 2024. Tapp is a 22-year veteran of the FBI and took charge of the San Antonio field office in 2024. SpaceX officials said the simpler approach should speed up the return to the moon, answering NASA's call to provide “acceleration approaches” to Artemis 3, the lunar mission currently contracted to SpaceX. subject: Maine Family Planning Ends Primary Care Services After Court Upholds Trump-Era Medicaid Funding Ban
body:
Here's the latest breaking news at The Maine Wire

** Maine Family Planning Ends Primary Care Services After Court Upholds Trump-Era Medicaid Funding Ban (https://mainepolicy.us9.list-manage.com/track/click?u=a975bf67c40a8d8be91ad606d&id=fc489fa786&e=f093fa355c)
------------------------------------------------------------
By Jon Fetherston on Oct 31, 2025 03:09 pm
One of Maine’s largest abortion care providers will end its primary care services on Friday after a federal court ruled that the Trump administration is not required to restore Medicaid funding to the organization. Maine Family Planning, which operates the state’s largest network of sexual and reproductive health clinics, announced that it will discontinue primary [...]

name: NEGOP
subject: REMINDER: 🚨 Your Weekly Update From The NEGOP
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY THE NEBRASKA REPUBLICAN PARTY](https://feyrpzhbb
body:
News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong.
| | | | |
---
| Letter from the Chair
---
Over the past several years, Nebraska Republicans have achieved remarkable success: we now hold all five Congressional seats and every statewide office. These hard-fought victories are a testament to the strength of our shared conservative values and the dedication of Republicans across the state.
Yet success is never permanent--and complacency is our greatest threat. When Republicans stay home, Democrats win. History proves it. Our work is far from finished, and we cannot afford to let up now.
To secure Nebraska's future, we must grow our party and expand participation. That starts with inviting more Republicans to get involved at the grassroots level--especially by attending our 2026 county conventions. These gatherings are where ideas take shape, leaders emerge, and our movement strengthens.
The path to continued dominance is clear:
* Identify more Nebraskans who share our conservative principles
* Engage them in the political process
* Mobilize every Republican to vote in every election

Every day, we remain focused on our core mission: promoting our platform and electing candidates who will fight for it. That requires mastering the fundamentals:
1. Raise money to fuel our efforts
2. Communicate our conservative message clearly and consistently
3. Recruit, develop, organize, and elect strong Republican leaders

I urge you to join us in this vital work. Donate to the Nebraska Republican Party today and get more involved--whether by attending a county convention, volunteering, or helping spread our message. Together, we'll defend our principles and elect Republicans up and down the ballot.
The future of Nebraska depends on it. Let's get to work.

Mary Jane Truemper Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party
---
| | [CONTRIBUTE TODAY](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001S9TKmR_hlSxOA2EZkMY59kgB9sNCq-6-8blkkuVqfj6yrrs2wZAUVi4-_CmsKw5c2aAnlhQZvochTjfLBM_yqg5R9GohudLbiwQruF3IC63bvYXnjqpWqOH5UjTmFv16ZDAR0V50Hq8TCkL48YPTSgrEYBDLuAfG&c=w1oAyPLiZLkj0wxjEY_HOrQxSi0VzuAd3kVmQARd7uu0vyrFW6FJMA==&ch=hcC9k7wT_5g6QTSph6jnCI6pwClnV9pYg3TXapu6S6GJEpyUHKtlTg==)
---
| |
---
| WHAT'S HAPPENING...
Candidate Recruitment Each week, I'm connecting with Nebraskans exploring public service, guiding committed candidates through next steps and equipping them with resources and tools, and partnering with community and party leaders, business owners, and elected officials to identify strong Republicans to run for offices up for election in 2026.
Recent highlights include two high-energy recruitment events in Lancaster and Douglas County, where attendees learned about seats up for election in 2026, candidate resources and tools provided to candidates by the NEGOP, and heard Senator Brad von Gillern share his journey into public office--resulting in several new candidate commitments.
The NEGOP Candidate Recruitment Committee has contacted over 300 Republican incumbents across federal, statewide, legislative, and multi-county offices to confirm re-election plans and is continuing to recruit challengers for Democrat-held seats. The enthusiasm is palpable--Nebraskans are ready to expand our majority.
We continue to host candidate trainings, like our upcoming Virtual Candidate Bootcamp series to equip potential candidates with the knowledge and confidence to run for office. These training sessions have also led to many new candidate commitments. Recruitment is a marathon and requires a multi-faceted approach, and we need your help. If you or someone you know is considering a run for any federal, statewide, legislative, and multi-county [office up for election in 2026](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001S9TKmR_hlSxOA2EZkMY59kgB9sNCq-6-8blkkuVqfj6yrrs2wZAUVh0ke6R5AnY7raugDmvs4J2G1nd90EtRq5hSOoShko46xlILUSa2yRioIQngNHK_gP9HYKkjSxjdSfNIHG6aqjBhvk7NwulQ8e2mpnLMMs7RCmNc9M2O8j7BBPCitipah6Pe8Xy7z7HL7SJnrB1Jq2FF4PPbZz1DHuxb5AdMdLZiQJjN0frvxObZik1CqJTLNWGPm9p0tS2bU-Vlu42ap2B4UIfNoTPxUk8du1A7CdZbQXdV7GXq08KldMN-XDW4aPrc6E0dn2hk3bazTXhcVsU=&c=w1oAyPLiZLkj0wxjEY_HOrQxSi0VzuAd3kVmQARd7uu0vyrFW6FJMA==&ch=hcC9k7wT_5g6QTSph6jnCI6pwClnV9pYg3TXapu6S6GJEpyUHKtlTg==), email me at [political@ne.gop](mailto:political@ne.gop)--I would love to discuss this opportunity and qualified candidates with you.
2026 Election Calendar Now Live
The official[ 2026 Nebraska Election Calendar](https://feyrpzhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=001S9TKmR_hlSxOA2EZkMY59kgB9sNCq-6-8blkkuVqfj6yrrs2wZAUVh0ke6R5AnY7hdtIsQpmVv12ygizhZjBPShBqeNrZQWYvhPkBWTcN3I1qost694IiM_lB4DFRgILMzgiAnA-1KJIPQaTVVyPENQPAJdVgUT5gJ4CY8hD24GXBvOPJ4qH-tm1adnDkjIb51Fk3stjUHuTff_N8TULk44nsaiN8K54oiDRZB5MTb3V5bne7UZQXqm0wBsa6lTR&c=w1oAyPLiZLkj0wxjEY_HOrQxSi0VzuAd3kVmQARd7uu0vyrFW6FJMA==&ch=hcC9k7wT_5g6QTSph6jnCI6pwClnV9pYg3TXapu6S6GJEpyUHKtlTg==) just became available on the Secretary of State's website this week. With references to Nebraska Revised Statutes, this calendar outlines all statutory deadlines for the May 12 Primary and November 3 General Election, including:
* Candidate Filing
* Voter Registration
* Early Voting
* Ballot Preparation
* Post-Election Processes

Key dates begin in November 2025 and run through December 2026--bookmark it now.
Your involvement is essential to electing principled Republicans who will defend our values and secure Nebraska's future. Encourage strong candidates to attend our training sessions, share recruitment leads, and stay informed using the official 2026 Nebraska Election Calendar.
As President Trump has said, "Together, we will finish the job of saving our country once and for all and raise the next generation of strong American Patriots and Leaders." So let's work together this election cycle and win up and down the ballot in 2026!
In Liberty,
Joe Hagerty Political Director, Nebraska Republican Party [political@ne.gop](mailto:political@ne.gop)
---

clean_body: News, events, and action items to keep Nebraska red and growing strong. | | | | | | Letter from the Chair Over the past several years, Nebraska Republicans have achieved remarkable success: we now hold all five Congressional seats and every statewide office. These hard-fought victories are a testament to the strength of our shared conservative values and the dedication of Republicans across the state. Yet success is never permanent--and complacency is our greatest threat. When Republicans stay home, Democrats win. History proves it. Our work is far from finished, and we cannot afford to let up now. To secure Nebraska's future, we must grow our party and expand participation. That starts with inviting more Republicans to get involved at the grassroots level--especially by attending our 2026 county conventions. These gatherings are where ideas take shape, leaders emerge, and our movement strengthens. The path to continued dominance is clear: Identify more Nebraskans who share our conservative principles Engage them in the political process Mobilize every Republican to vote in every election Every day, we remain focused on our core mission: promoting our platform and electing candidates who will fight for it. That requires mastering the fundamentals: 1. Raise money to fuel our efforts 2. Communicate our conservative message clearly and consistently 3. Recruit, develop, organize, and elect strong Republican leaders I urge you to join us in this vital work. Donate to the Nebraska Republican Party today and get more involved--whether by attending a county convention, volunteering, or helping spread our message. Together, we'll defend our principles and elect Republicans up and down the ballot. The future of Nebraska depends on it. Let's get to work. Mary Jane Truemper Chairman, Nebraska Republican Party | | == | | | WHAT'S HAPPENING... Candidate Recruitment Each week, I'm connecting with Nebraskans exploring public service, guiding committed candidates through next steps and equipping them with resources and tools, and partnering with community and party leaders, business owners, and elected officials to identify strong Republicans to run for offices up for election in 2026. Recent highlights include two high-energy recruitment events in Lancaster and Douglas County, where attendees learned about seats up for election in 2026, candidate resources and tools provided to candidates by the NEGOP, and heard Senator Brad von Gillern share his journey into public office--resulting in several new candidate commitments. The NEGOP Candidate Recruitment Committee has contacted over 300 Republican incumbents across federal, statewide, legislative, and multi-county offices to confirm re-election plans and is continuing to recruit challengers for Democrat-held seats. The enthusiasm is palpable--Nebraskans are ready to expand our majority. We continue to host candidate trainings, like our upcoming Virtual Candidate Bootcamp series to equip potential candidates with the knowledge and confidence to run for office. These training sessions have also led to many new candidate commitments. Recruitment is a marathon and requires a multi-faceted approach, and we need your help. If you or someone you know is considering a run for any federal, statewide, legislative, and multi-county ==, email me at mailto:political@ne.gop--I would love to discuss this opportunity and qualified candidates with you. 2026 Election Calendar Now Live The official== just became available on the Secretary of State's website this week. With references to Nebraska Revised Statutes, this calendar outlines all statutory deadlines for the May 12 Primary and November 3 General Election, including: Candidate Filing Voter Registration Early Voting Ballot Preparation Post-Election Processes Key dates begin in November 2025 and run through December 2026--bookmark it now. Your involvement is essential to electing principled Republicans who will defend our values and secure Nebraska's future. Encourage strong candidates to attend our training sessions, share recruitment leads, and stay informed using the official 2026 Nebraska Election Calendar. As President Trump has said, "Together, we will finish the job of saving our country once and for all and raise the next generation of strong American Patriots and Leaders." So let's work together this election cycle and win up and down the ballot in 2026! In Liberty, Joe Hagerty Political Director, Nebraska Republican Party mailto:political@ne.gop | | == | == | | == | | | UPCOMING NEGOP EVENTS | | | | == | | == | | == | == | | | Help Republicans win in 2026 and beyond. Fellow American, Let me be blunt. You are being lied to. They told you abortion was healthcare.
That it was safe. That it was rare. That it was compassionate. But today, more than 60 percent of abortions happen through a pill. Taken in silence. Usually at home. Often without a doctor. Often without the woman truly understanding what she is doing. The damage is staggering. To the baby. To the mother. To the culture. And most people are staying quiet. But one organization is not. That is why I am standing with PreBorn!
---
[**PreBorn! is doing what matters. They are saving lives. Not by yelling louder. Not through politics. But through love, truth, and real help.**](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJykkcGOnDAMhp_G3AbFcQjLgcN2WW5T9VCpRxSCh4kKSZSEqfr2FRJ9gj36s_X7k_5f9-lu3Ob8-t3s3Nt5SnkqT_67OV7MHI5i5pCKCz4mnicpZHNDvAk873w4YUh-wmrpbcdSzariHluNXddqpapnLxR2RIrsm0BlJT-4azshGxKEUjNXrj9TEQUiqkZR3T5YWdHpBxphCN9ACV5cCam2wWdOL1Pci-cUzGJNLrXnUm39s5SYgd5BjiDH1b24vuxqG3aQ4zWBHE2MbLYMNGYLNPz8EFo2Pz7vBFKbvQANjQCp1ww0ZJD6KPuUw5EsAw0xhRzZFufXa7Xz4o4daODduO2C1uzRuNUDDeWwvzn958EX9ueP98_bx7dWiRar1C_xj9s2l3ULSqxnzmldlS808urlvwAAAP__mEecdQ)

**They offer free ultrasounds to women in crisis.** They meet them where they are. They show them the reality of life inside the womb. **They walk with those women for up to two years after the baby is born.** **They share the Gospel.** They do not abandon women. They stay. Here is what stopped me. It costs just $28 to provide a free ultrasound. That is it.
$28 to help save a baby's life.
$140 to help rescue five. **[_Click here to give now_ _to support this incredible work >>_](https://email.editor.conservativebroadcast.net/c/eJykkcGOnDAMhp_G3AbFcQjLgcN2WW5T9VCpRxSCh4kKSZSEqfr2FRJ9gj36s_X7k_5f9-lu3Ob8-t3s3Nt5SnkqT_67OV7MHI5i5pCKCz4mnicpZHNDvAk873w4YUh-wmrpbcdSzariHluNXddqpapnLxR2RIrsm0BlJT-4azshGxKEUjNXrj9TEQUiqkZR3T5YWdHpBxphCN9ACV5cCam2wWdOL1Pci-cUzGJNLrXnUm39s5SYgd5BjiDH1b24vuxqG3aQ4zWBHE2MbLYMNGYLNPz8EFo2Pz7vBFKbvQANjQCp1ww0ZJD6KPuUw5EsAw0xhRzZFufXa7Xz4o4daODduO2C1uzRuNUDDeWwvzn958EX9ueP98_bx7dWiRar1C_xj9s2l3ULSqxnzmldlS808urlvwAAAP__mEecdQ)_ _** You and I both know the abortion industry has everything on its side. The media. The government. The money. The silence of people who should speak up. But we still have the truth.
We still have a voice.
And we still have the ability to act. If you want to fight back against a culture of death, this is how you do it. In a real and practical way. Support PreBorn!
Give what you can.
Help save a life today. Sincerely,
Tucker Carlson

They walk with those women for up to two years after the baby is born. They share the Gospel. They do not abandon women. They stay. Here is what stopped me. It costs just $28 to provide a free ultrasound. That is it. $28 to help save a baby's life. $140 to help rescue five. You and I both know the abortion industry has everything on its side. The media. The government. The money. The silence of people who should speak up. But we still have the truth. We still have a voice. And we still have the ability to act. If you want to fight back against a culture of death, this is how you do it. In a real and practical way. Support PreBorn! Give what you can. Help save a life today. Sincerely, Tucker Carlson Pop Acta Media | Conservative Broadcast PO Box 2221 Fort Collins, CO 80522 |

subject: Pennsylvania has fallen
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY DEFEND OUR COURTS, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE
body:
3 days remain until E-day and we are scrambling to contain MAGA’s breakthrough.
| | | | | [](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0012x9-pDtngsI1-g1BYSFKLoB-9D8YqHcZ3tvEgMgIQKI7s1RN3lz_BXOVRbNOP4ogd1B8JZDaLOWb9YVCrSq1rKkEdGL_WzP7KuL5Rk0biXzYqDX3rRsfc_1atEhqBVgmIZrwZ7kNphHRzEnQhkBkcfOZMlSBAsJ8if5HNWUKxlsmV7H_5RfoVrsFBFyVTYwrKWyFQ0VaLQurul0ejYzrl3uLHhplOBkwD9fiC8mRGp0=&c=iST8N2N9K53snJyHMJ5Du1d07k_-TqxR5fg5kr8did4qNneGoLQqtw==&ch=MjUeTq3mVXbRsiiHxAwsK4cGnUTsHpJwLF2Ze-iFJO-lRhVmZyAhQw==)

|
Peter,
The situation for Democrats in the Pennsylvania State Supreme Court election just went from bad to critical.
The lights are flashing red. Democrats are inadvertently voting for the MAGA ballot option by the tens of thousands, a rate so alarming that it could decide the election. Here's how it happened:
First \- this Summer the GOP set their sights on a hostile takeover of the Pennsylvania State Supreme Court. Control of this court means that MAGA can impose an extreme abortion ban, redraw PA's Congressional map, and rig the 2026 and 2028 elections. We'll never win PA again.
Then \- the GOP put millions of dollars behind a disinfo campaign using sentiments typically expressed by Dems such as "Defend Democracy, Protect Women, or No Kings - Term limit the court" in order to deceive Democrats and independents to vote No, the MAGA ballot option.
Now \- Thousands of Democratic and Independent voters who have turned in their mail in ballots are saying they fell for it \- they got duped and voted to remove Pennsylvania's 3 Democratic justices because of MAGA's disinformation.
If trends continue at these alarming rates, we lose. The headlines will read: "Pennsylvania has fallen."
3 days remain until E-day and we are scrambling to contain MAGA's breakthrough. We refuse to let good Democrats be deceived.
This past Wednesday we placed a $300,000 cable buy and on Friday AM we put another $200,000 behind hyper targeted digital ads both of which tell voters that voting Yes is how we protect abortion.
It's a start but we can't let it be too little too late. [The cost per vote in this election (how much it costs to get a Dem to the polls on average) is $55. Can we count on you to "buy" a vote in this election by rushing a donation of $55 or whatever you can spare?](https://yfq78zhbb.cc.rs6.net/tn.jsp?f=0012x9-pDtngsI1-g1BYSFKLoB-9D8YqHcZ3tvEgMgIQKI7s1RN3lz_BXOVRbNOP4ogd1B8JZDaLOWb9YVCrSq1rKkEdGL_WzP7KuL5Rk0biXzYqDX3rRsfc_1atEhqBVgmIZrwZ7kNphHRzEnQhkBkcfOZMlSBAsJ8if5HNWUKxlsmV7H_5RfoVrsFBFyVTYwrKWyFQ0VaLQurul0ejYzrl2T3f7ezwBVRqw-broykGBg=&c=iST8N2N9K53snJyHMJ5Du1d07k_-TqxR5fg5kr8did4qNneGoLQqtw==&ch=MjUeTq3mVXbRsiiHxAwsK4cGnUTsHpJwLF2Ze-iFJO-lRhVmZyAhQw==)

Peter,
The situation for Democrats in the Pennsylvania State Supreme Court election just went from bad to critical.
The lights are flashing red. Democrats are inadvertently voting for the MAGA ballot option by the tens of thousands, a rate so alarming that it could decide the election. Here's how it happened:
First \- this Summer the GOP set their sights on a hostile takeover of the Pennsylvania State Supreme Court. Control of this court means that MAGA can impose an extreme abortion ban, redraw PA's Congressional map, and rig the 2026 and 2028 elections. We'll never win PA again.
Then \- the GOP put millions of dollars behind a disinfo campaign using sentiments typically expressed by Dems such as "Defend Democracy, Protect Women, or No Kings - Term limit the court" in order to deceive Democrats and independents to vote No, the MAGA ballot option.
Now \- Thousands of Democratic and Independent voters who have turned in their mail in ballots are saying they fell for it \- they got duped and voted to remove Pennsylvania's 3 Democratic justices because of MAGA's disinformation.
If trends continue at these alarming rates, we lose. The headlines will read: "Pennsylvania has fallen."
3 days remain until E-day and we are scrambling to contain MAGA's breakthrough. We refuse to let good Democrats be deceived.
This past Wednesday we placed a $300,000 cable buy and on Friday AM we put another $200,000 behind hyper targeted digital ads both of which tell voters that voting Yes is how we protect abortion.

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

name: Douglas Carswell
subject: Governor & Speaker voice strong support for school choice!
body:
| | | [View this email in your browser](https://mailchi.mp/mspolicy/leaderssupportschoolchoice?e=5703353baa)
---
| |
---
| _**If you're not in front of our Saturday email, you're behind.**_
---
| Dear Friend,
The Mississippi Center for Public Policy hosted a "Celebration of Mississippi Success" last week to showcase the state's recent free-market reforms.

**The big news is that both Governor Tate Reeves and Speaker Jason White spoke at the gathering, voicing strong support for universal school choice in Mississippi and declaring it their top priority for the 2026 legislative session.**

Here are some of the highlights!
---
|
---
_There was the Governor and plenty of glamour_
| **Governor Tate Reeves** joined us for a candid conversation with our President and CEO, Douglas Carswell, where he discussed Mississippi's strong economic momentum and the conservative principles fueling our growth. **He went further in his support for school choice than I have heard him before! **
---
|
---
_Speaker Jason White on his focus for the 2026 legislative session_
| **Speaker of the House Jason White** shared his vision for the 2026 Legislative Session, emphasizing school choice as a top priority for Mississippi families.

The keynote address by **Rich Lowry, editor of National Review** , was a standout moment. He praised Mississippi as a national model for how pro-growth, pro-family policies can drive renewal across the country.
---
|
---
_Rich Lowry and plenty of patriotism_
| Mississippi is proving that conservative principles work - lower taxes, limited government, and personal freedom are creating real opportunities for families and businesses alike.
Your continued support makes our ongoing work possible. Thank you!
---
|
---
_Celebrating fun, friends and free markets!_
| **Over the coming days, I will be sure to share with you highlights of what our guests had to say, including details from Speaker Jason White on what to expect in terms of education reform in the 2026 session. **

Exciting times ahead!
---
| [Forward this email to a friend!](https://mspolicy.us18.list-manage.com/track/click?u=60d4052c66b6745831ccdf7c8&id=ca8fd198ee&e=5703353baa "Forward this email to a friend!")
---
| |
---
Warmest regards,

Douglas Carswell
President & CEO

clean_body: | | | | | | If you're not in front of our Saturday email, you're behind. | Dear Friend, The Mississippi Center for Public Policy hosted a "Celebration of Mississippi Success" last week to showcase the state's recent free-market reforms. The big news is that both Governor Tate Reeves and Speaker Jason White spoke at the gathering, voicing strong support for universal school choice in Mississippi and declaring it their top priority for the 2026 legislative session. Here are some of the highlights! | There was the Governor and plenty of glamour | Governor Tate Reeves joined us for a candid conversation with our President and CEO, Douglas Carswell, where he discussed Mississippi's strong economic momentum and the conservative principles fueling our growth. He went further in his support for school choice than I have heard him before! | Speaker Jason White on his focus for the 2026 legislative session | Speaker of the House Jason White shared his vision for the 2026 Legislative Session, emphasizing school choice as a top priority for Mississippi families. The keynote address by Rich Lowry, editor of National Review , was a standout moment. He praised Mississippi as a national model for how pro-growth, pro-family policies can drive renewal across the country. | Rich Lowry and plenty of patriotism | Mississippi is proving that conservative principles work - lower taxes, limited government, and personal freedom are creating real opportunities for families and businesses alike. Your continued support makes our ongoing work possible. Thank you! | Celebrating fun, friends and free markets! | Over the coming days, I will be sure to share with you highlights of what our guests had to say, including details from Speaker Jason White on what to expect in terms of education reform in the 2026 session. Exciting times ahead! | "Forward this email to a friend!" | | Warmest regards, Douglas Carswell President & CEO | | |

| | Dorian Gray glanced at the picture, and suddenly an uncontrollable feeling of hatred for Basil Hallward came over him, as though it had been suggested to him by the image on the canvas, whispered into his ear by those grinning lips. The mad passions of a hunted animal stirred within him, and he loathed the man who was seated at the table, more than in his whole life he had ever loathed anything. He glanced wildly around. Something glimmered on the top of the painted chest that faced him. His eye fell on it. He knew what it was. It was a knife that he had brought up, some days before, to cut a piece of cord, and had forgotten to take away with him. He moved slowly towards it, passing Hallward as he did so. As soon as he got behind him, he seized it and turned round. Hallward stirred in his chair as if he was going to rise. He rushed at him and dug the knife into the great vein that is behind the ear, crushing the man’s head down on the table and stabbing again and again. There was a stifled groan and the horrible sound of some one choking with blood. Three times the outstretched arms shot up convulsively, waving grotesque, stiff-fingered hands in the air. He stabbed him twice more, but the man did not move. Something began to trickle on the floor. He waited for a moment, still pressing the head down. Then he threw the knife on the table, and listened. He could hear nothing, but the drip, drip on the threadbare carpet. He opened the door and went out on the landing. The house was absolutely quiet. No one was about. For a few seconds he stood bending over the balustrade and peering down into the black seething well of darkness. Then he took out the key and returned to the room, locking himself in as he did so. The thing was still seated in the chair, straining over the table with bowed head, and humped back, and long fantastic arms. Had it not been for the red jagged tear in the neck and the clotted black pool that was slowly widening on the table, one would have said that the man was simply asleep. How quickly it had all been done! He felt strangely calm, and walking over to the window, opened it and stepped out on the balcony. The wind had blown the fog away, and the sky was like a monstrous peacock’s tail, starred with myriads of golden eyes. He looked down and saw the policeman going his rounds and flashing the long beam of his lantern on the doors of the silent houses. The crimson spot of a prowling hansom gleamed at the corner and then vanished. A woman in a fluttering shawl was creeping slowly by the railings, staggering as she went. Now and then she stopped and peered back. Once, she began to sing in a hoarse voice. The policeman strolled over and said something to her. She stumbled away, laughing. A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him.
---

Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671.

A bitter blast swept across the square. The gas-lamps flickered and became blue, and the leafless trees shook their black iron branches to and fro. He shivered and went back, closing the window behind him. | | | One new card quietly delivers what Elon Musk called “the future of money.” While most cards still charge sky-high interest... Atlantic Ave., Suite 200 Delray Beach, FL 33444 Ivan Dmytrovych Sirko was born in the early seventeenth century, approximately between 1605 and 1610. This is evidenced primarily by the results of research on Sirko's remains, according to which the Cossack ataman was 70-75 years old at the time of his death in 1680. The first researcher of Sirko's biography, the famous Ukrainian historian Dmytro Yavornytskyi, suggested that the future ataman was born in Sloboda Ukraine in the settlement of Merefa (now the city of Merefa, Kharkiv region). However, this is not true. Merefa was founded only in 1658, i.e. when Sirko was in his late teens. However, Sirko and his family lived in Merefa for a long time in his house, and after his death, his wife and some family members lived there. The fact that Merefa in Slobozhanshchyna is associated with the name of the Cossack Ataman is evidenced by another name for the town recorded in some Ukrainian chronicles, Sirkivka. Where, then, should we look for Sirko's homeland? In our opinion, the sources give grounds to localize it in Eastern Podillia, namely in the Vinnytsia region. It is worth recalling that Sirko was a colonel in Vinnytsia (Kalnytsia) in 1658-1660. As you know, at that time in Ukraine, people who came from the local area were usually elected to such positions. Let us also recall a letter from Hetman of the Left Bank Ukraine Ivan Samoilovych to the tsarist voivode Prince G. Romodanovsky of April 20, 1675*, which recounts (in Russian) Sirko's own words: "And since I was born among the Poles, I want to die there." Thus, it is clear that Sirko was born on the Ukrainian lands that were under the rule of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, not Muscovy (the latter included Slobozhanshchyna). The historian Volodymyr Borysenko's assumption that Sirko's birthplace was the hundred-man town of the Bratslav regiment Murafa, a few kilometers from Kalnyk, is correct. Now it is the village of Zhdanov, Vinnytsia region. By the way, Murafa in the Slobozhanshchyna probably originated from this Murafa, because immigrants from the Right Bank of Ukraine to the Left Bank often gave the newly founded settlements the names of their native villages or towns. It is also worth noting Sirko's great interest in Keleberda, which he founded in 1675 on the left bank of the Dnipro. For a long time, he sought from the Moscow tsar the right to own this town or sloboda, as well as the strategically important crossing of the Dnipro near Perevolochna (Sirko wanted this crossing not for himself, but for the entire Zaporozhian Lowland Army). Contrary to popular belief, we believe that Sirko did not come from a Cossack family, but from a small Ukrainian Orthodox gentry. For example, we know the name of a Podillia nobleman, Voytekh Sirko, who was married to a certain Olena Kozynska. Sources mention them in 1592. It is possible that they were relatives of the Cossack leader. Perhaps it is no coincidence that the kings of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, writing to Sirko, called him "urodzon." The word "nobleman" evokes negative associations for many people due to stereotypes instilled in school textbooks of the Stalinist era. However, this approach is not always correct. The term "nobleman" is identical to the words "knight," "chevalier," "nobleman," etc. and was commonly used not only in Poland but also in Ukraine, Belarus, and the Czech Republic. If scholars in Russia, Poland, and the Czech Republic, having contracted vulgar sociologism, no longer accuse Pushkin or Bunin, Suvorov or Kutuzov, Žižka or Kostiushko of being of noble or gentry origin, why have attacks on the Ukrainian and Belarusian gentry continued until recently? Is it not because in this way the imperial "divide and conquer" was transferred to our long-suffering history? Was it not because it was necessary to impose a nihilistic attitude toward the history of Ukraine, to "prove" that Ukrainians or Belarusians were just dark labor without national consciousness, ethnographic material doomed to build other people's empires? Meanwhile, the Ukrainian gentry produced many outstanding sons of Ukraine, fighters for its freedom. Suffice it to mention the names of Kryshtof Kosinski, Petro Konashevych-Sahaidachny, Ivan Sulyma, Ivan Mazepa, Bohdan Khmelnytsky, and his associates: Ivan and Danylo Vyhovsky, Ivan Bohun, Danylo and Ivan Nechai, Mykhailo Krychevsky, Jan Sokolovsky, Siluyan Muzhylovsky, and others. Sirko's appearance and personality do not always coincide with the conventional ideas about him that have developed in some works of art. The Cossack leader was slightly taller than average (174-176 cm), had regular facial features, and a straight nose. By the way, the great Relin intuitively caught this when he depicted him in the center of his painting "Cossacks Writing a Letter to the Turkish Sultan." It is interesting that the prototype for Sirko's image in the painting was the famous military leader General Dragomirov. Only one significant detail was not known to Repin and his consultant D. Yavornytskyi - a red birthmark on Sirko's lower lip on the right side. His contemporaries considered it a "sign of God" given to distinguish him from ordinary people. The Grand Hetman of the Crown, the future King of the Polish-Lithuanian Commonwealth, Jan Sobieski, who knew Sirko well and met him in Bratslav in 1671. | | You are receiving this newsletter from YourCapitalMinds.com, an esteemed member of the Stark Media LLC family. Today, I will share my vision for a stronger, safer, and more affordable Virginia.
It's a vision informed by the thousands of conversations I've had with my fellow Virginians: students unsure how they will find a job; couples unsure how they will buy a home; parents unsure how they will pay for childcare and senior care at the same time.
_It's also a vision that stands in stark contrast with that offered by Winsome Earle-Sears._
My opponent has stood in lockstep with Donald Trump's extreme agenda, from supporting an abortion ban, to backing attacks on Virginia's economy and firing workers, to slashing resources for our hospitals and public schools.
_As Governor, I will put Virginians first — no matter who's in the White House._
But we still have a lot of work left to do to drive our message home with voters.
[_**So, can I count on you to contribute $25 or more to help us flip the Governor's office from red to blue? clean_body: Today, I will share my vision for a stronger, safer, and more affordable Virginia. = In just a couple of hours, I'll hold a rally with President Barack Obama in Norfolk. This is the biggest campaign event in the biggest election of 2025, and I'm respectfully asking you to join our donor list before I take the stage. = = = = = = = Today, I will share my vision for a stronger, safer, and more affordable Virginia. It's a vision informed by the thousands of conversations I've had with my fellow Virginians: students unsure how they will find a job; couples unsure how they will buy a home; parents unsure how they will pay for childcare and senior care at the same time. It's also a vision that stands in stark contrast with that offered by Winsome Earle-Sears. My opponent has stood in lockstep with Donald Trump's extreme agenda, from supporting an abortion ban, to backing attacks on Virginia's economy and firing workers, to slashing resources for our hospitals and public schools. As Governor, I will put Virginians first — no matter who's in the White House. But we still have a lot of work left to do to drive our message home with voters. = = = = = = = Thank you for always having my back, Abigail Spanberger By submitting your phone number you're agreeing to receive periodic text messages from the Spanberger for Governor campaign. name: Wesley Bell
subject: Keep on keeping on
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY BELL FOR MISSOURI
body:
I'll always stand up for the people who keep this country running, no matter what. Peter,

Thanks to this incredible team, we hit our end-of-month fundraising goal for October — _and I couldn't be more grateful._

Look, it's not lost on me that times are tough, and there's a lot on people's plates right now.

We're in the middle of an avoidable government shutdown thanks to the president and extreme Republicans in Congress, all while prices keep rising and families are staring down bills that feel impossible to manage.

**That's why this fight matters so much, and why I'm beyond thankful to everyone on this team.**

Together, we're fighting to ensure no one has to choose between paying rent and putting food on the table. In our district alone, tens of thousands of our neighbors relying on food assistance to make ends meet might not get benefits, and more than 10,000 federal workers have already missed paychecks because of Washington's dysfunction. **This shutdown impacts all of us.**

Peter, and this is what we can't forget: The GOP is counting on us to give up. They're hoping we'll run out of energy, out of resources, and out of hope. But we're building a movement powered by folks who know what it means to sacrifice, who believe that when times are hard, we pull together, not apart.

Thank you for always stepping up when it matters most,

Wesley

**P.S. Your commitment to our cause is what drives us forward, and we're grateful for your support. We reviewed your questionnaire, and we noticed you left Question 5 blank. I know it was an honest oversight, but I want to make sure your voice is fully heard. That's why I'm asking if you could review and re-submit your responses by 11:59PM tonight. You're one of our most valued allies in the fight to protect fair elections, and your input is crucial to our success. By taking a minute to revisit your census, you'll be helping us build a stronger, more just America. Thank you again for standing with us, Peter. You're making a real difference. | Paid for by the NRSC. Not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee. |

name: Karen Meredith
subject: Gold Star Parents
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS
body:
I became a Gold Star mother because my son was sent off to a war we didn’t need to fight. And as a Gold Star mother, watching Trump and Hegseth lead our military into another needless war is difficult to see.
#
My name is Karen Meredith, I am a Gold Star mother, and my only child, 1LT Ken Ballard, is buried at Arlington National Cemetery.
Ken joined the Army after graduating from high school in 1995. As an Army granddaughter, Army daughter, Army sister, and Army mom, I couldn't be more proud of his decision.
My son deployed to Iraq in May of 2003, and 384 days later, he was killed while fighting an Iranian-backed militia in Najaf. He had been scheduled to return home eight days earlier, but his tour was extended.
I became a Gold Star mother because my son was sent off to a war we didn’t need to fight. And as a Gold Star mother, watching Trump and Hegseth attempt to lead our military into another needless war is painful.
How many more Gold Star families will there be if we send American troops into Venezuela? Are Trump and Hegseth even thinking about that? No. To them, soldiers like Ken are toys they play with to look tough.
It can be hard to know what to do at moments like these. For me, I have been active in the Gold Star and Veteran's communities as an advocate for Military families. And for those families, I am going to do all that I can to stop Trump’s next Forever War before it starts.
That’s why I am proud to be a part of the VoteVets community, and why I am writing to you today.
[**I’m asking you to sign on with nearly 10,000 other VoteVets supporters to tell Donald Trump that America doesn’t need or want another war. No more Forever Wars. No more Gold Star families.**](https://act.votevets.org/signup/2511_vv_forever_war/?source=em251101-1-120&akid=34742%2E3013755%2EYXUjsP)
| [NO MORE FOREVER WARS](https://act.votevets.org/signup/2511_vv_forever_war/?source=em251101-1-120&akid=34742%2E3013755%2EYXUjsP)
---
I’m grateful you took the time to read this email, and I hope you’ll take the next step and sign your name.
Thank you,
Karen Meredith
Gold Star Families Liaison
VoteVets.org

PAID FOR BY VOTEVETS, VOTEVETS.ORG, NOT AUTHORIZED BY ANY CANDIDATE OR CANDIDATE’S COMMITTEE
Use of military rank, job titles or photographs in uniform does not imply the endorsement of the Department of Defense, Departments of the Army, Navy, Air Force, or the Marines or Space Force.
This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com . Email is a very important way we communicate with supporters like you, but if you want to, you can [ unsubscribe here ](https://act.votevets.org/unsubscribe/unsubscribe?t=1&akid=34742%2E3013755%2EYXUjsP).

body:
No one would believe us... | | |
---
| | [](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118445685/579795139/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_251031RateB_JZ_11_NO_NG_N_LOGO&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhmMTFiY2RkLTI2YjctZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=iE8Kq8BTIOnKG25jdXmBfYvVHn3tPSjyKxZaMSH4bj4=)

Peter,

Many of you have written to us that you feel a sense of despair when you think about the future of American politics. So much corruption. So much awful policy. Where are the “adults in the room” or the “leaders with integrity”....

But here is some much-needed GREAT and ENCOURAGING news: Over the past 12 months, Her Bold Move has supported around 40 candidates in primaries and special elections ACROSS AMERICA.

In 12 months, **only 1 candidate** from the entire Her Bold Move roster **has _lost_ a race.** That means we’ve won more than 97% of the races we’ve been involved with since Election Day 2024.

Here’s the thing: “Win rate” often comes with different implications – and it can be used to pressure orgs to support candidates who already had a high likelihood of winning based on fundraising numbers, incumbency advantage, or the political leaning of their district.

But at Her Bold Move, we see it as proof that our programs, **_specifically designed to support women candidates running in the _toughest_ _races_ in the country –_** are working.

[**Her Bold Move has repeatedly changed the outcomes of elections. We support candidates running for seats in majority male rooms and/or in states with abortion bans. In many cases, we’re our candidates’ only national endorsement – but we still help them beat the odds AND WIN! You can be a part of this uniquely impactful movement for women by rushing $20.25 RIGHT NOW. Let’s keep up this incredible momentum before Election Day 2025 – happening THIS TUESDAY!**](https://click.ngpvan.com/k/118445687/579795147/1701071684?refcode=EM_F_251031RateB_JZ_11_NO_NG_N_L1&amount=20.25&nvep=ew0KICAiVGVuYW50VXJpIjogIm5ncHZhbjovL3Zhbi9FQS9FQTAwNy8xLzg4NDk1IiwNCiAgIkRpc3RyaWJ1dGlvblVuaXF1ZUlkIjogIjhmMTFiY2RkLTI2YjctZjAxMS04ZTYxLTYwNDViZGVkOGJhNCIsDQogICJFbWFpbEFkZHJlc3MiOiAiZHB3aWxsaXM2N0BnbWFpbC5jb20iDQp9&hmac=iE8Kq8BTIOnKG25jdXmBfYvVHn3tPSjyKxZaMSH4bj4=)
